upgrade_source: Wikipedia (Accessibility of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority; Canarsie-Rockaway Parkway station); MTA press release (2020 improvements)
notes: Ground-level terminal station. One of only four wheelchair-accessible stations in the subway system before 1988, accessible by virtue of its street-level design. In 2020, MTA completed a three-part project ($5.48M) to enhance accessibility: relocated station agent booths, extended/retrofitted platform boarding areas with warning strips, reduced platform gaps, installed new ADA-compliant ramp, new lighting, signage, and PA system. Using pre-1988 as original accessible date.
